spec.reasoning: Two anchor types used: lexical (renaming columns and sheets) and distractor_column. Column renames maintain same descriptiveness: 'Unique ID' → 'UID' (abbreviation), 'Function' → 'Dept' (abbreviation), 'Class' → 'Category' (full word to full word). Both 'Result what i am getting' and 'Result what i am expecting' sheets get the same column renames to preserve alignment. Sheet renames change the result sheets but NOT 'Main unique ID' since that name appears directly in the instruction text as a key reference. The distractor column 'UID_Check' is plausibly related to the UID column but clearly different. Risk flags note multi_sheet_references and row insertion dependencies, so no title_row_insert is used. The instruction references 'Main unique ID', 'Result what I am getting', and 'Result what I am expecting' by name — the latter two sheet renames will require the agent to find sheets by actual name rather than hardcoded old names. Note: 'Function' and 'Class' do not appear as common English words in the instruction body in a way that would conflict with renaming.
